title: haplopappus hirtus subsp. typicus
intro: Haplopappus hirtus subsp. typicus, also known as the prickly golden bush or prickly golden aster, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Haplopappus hirtus subsp. typicus include soothing and calming effects on the skin, reducing inflammation and pain, and providing antioxidant protection against cell damage. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for its medicinal and health benefits, and is often used in herbal remedies to treat skin irritations and wounds.
